latest_daily.title: What landed today, 2026-06-13
latest_daily.summary: Three new cards, no new operators. April Dunford argues that AI market uncertainty has made vendor point of view the missing ingredient in enterprise sales. Kevin Indig publishes data showing algorithm-gifted search visibility reverts while authority-backed gains hold. Simon Willison offers a practical proxy for comparing model training depth.

recent_daily.title: What landed today, 2026-06-13
recent_daily.summary: Three new cards, no new operators. April Dunford argues that AI market uncertainty has made vendor point of view the missing ingredient in enterprise sales. Kevin Indig publishes data showing algorithm-gifted search visibility reverts while authority-backed gains hold. Simon Willison offers a practical proxy for comparing model training depth.

recent_daily.title: What landed today, 2026-06-12
recent_daily.summary: Five new cards, no new operators. Boris Cherny's Fortune interview series (June 8-11) reveals how cascading agent architecture works in production, how to benchmark AI ROI against labor rather than software, and what happens to code review when code writing is solved. Simon Willison documents frontier agent breadth-first problem decomposition. Rand Fishkin publishes 2026 data showing zero-click search has crossed 68% and explains why it will not reverse.

recent_daily.title: What landed today, 2026-06-11
recent_daily.summary: Four new insight cards, no new operators. Two themes emerged from this week's operator output: frontier AI shifting the human role from active steering to commissioning and judging, and the production-system implications of routing and transparency choices.

recent_daily.title: What landed today, 2026-06-09
recent_daily.summary: Three new insight cards, one new operator. Two themes emerged from this week's operator output: making AI adoption organizationally sustainable, and how Google's May 2026 Core Update reset visibility around source type fit rather than authority alone.

recent_daily.title: What landed today, 2026-06-04
recent_daily.summary: Five new insight cards, one new operator. Two themes from the AI engineering lane and one from future-of-work converged this week on the economics and safety of running agents in production.

recent_daily.title: What landed today, 2026-05-27
recent_daily.summary: Five new insight cards, two new operators, one new synthesis pattern. Three independent specialists named structural failures in their respective lanes within 48 hours of each other.

recent_daily.title: What landed today, 2026-05-25
recent_daily.summary: Four new insights, zero new operators, three pattern updates. The AEO citation universe split into two distinct populations. Verification remained the rate limiter across every domain AI accelerated.

recent_daily.title: Five insights, two themes. AI search rewired at the platform level. VOC craft is the answer.
recent_daily.summary: Five new cards across five operators. Two themes: AI search architecture changed at the platform level (Mike King, Rand Fishkin, Barry Schwartz), and content that resonates is built from verbatim buyer language not synthesis (Maja Voje, Nicolas Cole).

recent_daily.title: One level down: two operators on why surface-level matching misses what matters
recent_daily.summary: Two new insights, two synthesis patterns updated, one new operator. One meta-theme ran through both: surface-level matching fails where depth is needed. Shreyas Doshi names it for strategy. Anthropic names it for security.

recent_daily.title: AEO splits into three jobs, the judgment ceiling holds, and listening compounds
recent_daily.summary: Nine new cards. AEO fractures into citation, traffic, and IP protection as three separate jobs. The judgment ceiling holds as execution costs drop. One strong data set on customer marketing as systematic listening.

recent_daily.title: Five new insights: recovery floors, AI consensus, and task-driven minimalism
recent_daily.summary: Five new cards from five operators. Rewind names the chilling effect that recovery gaps create on AI adoption. Packy McCormick and Nicolas Cole converge on distinctiveness as the post-AI scarcity from independent angles. Pawel Huryn and Chris Orlob arrive at the same minimalist discipline applied to skill acquisition and outreach sequencing.

recent_daily.title: Earn the citation, hold the floor, retire the position metric
recent_daily.summary: Four new cards from Lily Ray, Aleyda Solis, Eli Schwartz, and Aakash Gupta. Ray and Solis converge on the structural architecture of AEO visibility from opposite directions. Schwartz names the measurement construct that is dissolving beneath the whole category. Gupta gives a one-sentence test that changes how you scope a skills library.

recent_daily.title: The reliability trap, stripping what does not compound, and context isolation
recent_daily.summary: Seven new cards, one new pattern, three pattern extensions. Two themes surfaced independently across the week: reliability quietly degrading the oversight it depends on, and AI abundance shifting the scarce resource from output to judgment.

recent_daily.title: AI implementation realism and AEO benchmarks, 2026-05-11
recent_daily.summary: Four new cards across two themes: human checkpoints as the AI implementation separator (Pierri + Breunig convergence extending pat_verification-as-human-job), and AI discovery producing measurable signup volume (Poyar benchmark + Schwartz on Google's formalized citation surfaces). Two new operators: Drew Breunig, Barry Schwartz.

recent_daily.title: Context engineering quantified, AEO continuity, volume versus curation
recent_daily.summary: Four new cards from the week of May 10, 2026. Poyar and Voje survey 200 GTM operators and produce the first large-sample quantitative evidence for the context-not-capability thesis. Lily Ray frames AEO as an extension of SEO discipline, not a replacement. Swyx names curation as the quality lever in the AI-volume era. Anthropic Routines establishes the boundary between cloud-hosted and local agent execution.

recent_daily.title: AEO becomes a page-experience problem; agentic architecture settles into named patterns
recent_daily.summary: Eleven new cards, one new synthesis pattern, two pattern updates, four new operators. Four AEO practitioners converged on a unified experience-and-structure gate frame. Three themes from independent directions.

recent_daily.title: Verification convergence: four operators, one platform answer
recent_daily.summary: Nine new cards plus one extended pattern. The week's dominant signal: closing the verification loop is the production bottleneck in AI-native work. It converged across four independent operators, and Anthropic shipped the platform version of the same argument.

recent_daily.title: Verification, judgement, scarcity, voice, three converging frames in one week
recent_daily.summary: 15 new cards, 2 synthesis patterns, 4 new operators. Today's batch plus a back-audit of past digests (April 26 to May 4) for atomic claims that hadn't yet been ingested.
sources: Backfill audit, past digests April 26 to May 4

recent_daily.title: Research scan v1-v6, depth-pilot to new-operator workflow
recent_daily.summary: Six research-scan batches ran across the day, ingesting **134** insight cards, **13** synthesis patterns, **4** methodology playbooks, **2** contradictions, and **2** new operator profiles. The batches followed a depth-first protocol, fewer operators per pass, more substantive cards per operator, and capped with the new-operator workflow that produced the David Ogilvy and Dave Trott profiles.
sources: Research scan v5, copywriting cluster batch (5 operators)
Research scan v6, new-operator workflow (David Ogilvy + Dave Trott)

recent_daily.title: Quality pass, 79 cards rewritten / stub-flagged
recent_daily.summary: A quality pass on the corpus. 79 cards (verified from `git log --diff-filter=M`) were modified, shallow ones rewritten with substantive primary-source quotes and stronger mechanism sections; borderline ones stub-flagged for a follow-up. Two specific re-attribution fixes for Sam Mallikarjunan and Sourav Mohanty. Only 1 new card landed; the day was about depth, not adds.

recent_daily.title: Site rebuild + data triage, 216 cards, 182 operator profiles
recent_daily.summary: Phase 1-3 site rebuild (per-view UX redesign, pattern radial converge viz + constellation mobile map, domain pages as mini-anthologies) plus three rounds of layout audit fixes. Three phases of data triage closed gaps the new UI exposed, fixed YAML parser, deduped 3 confirmed duplicates, patched 19 dead URLs and 16 operator externals, restored LinkedIn reactions, and ingested 182 operator profiles + 216 cards. Plus 19 card modifications.

recent_daily.title: Pass C orphan ingestion + first synthesis patterns + Curiosity backfill
recent_daily.summary: The biggest single ingest day in the corpus's life. 318 insight cards, 117 new operator profiles, 24 synthesis patterns, 9 contradictions, and 23 methodology playbooks all landed, on top of 286 card modifications from a launch-ready scrub. Pass C orphan-ingestion ran in 6 batches; Curiosity backfill ran in 8 batches adding external links across hundreds of profiles; the audit pass closed 8 stubs, validated 30 URLs, repaired 73 cards.

recent_daily.title: Phase 1, codex foundation built
recent_daily.summary: The day the corpus started. After two months of repository-only existence, the codex was reframed as a cross-domain operator-insight library. 109 insight cards and 174 operator profiles landed across 30+ ingest commits. Boris Cherny, Simon Willison, Cat Wu, April Dunford, Andy Raskin, Elena Verna, Brian Halligan, Asha Sharma, and many more got their first cards. The watchOS-inspired knowledge graph UI shipped alongside.

recent_daily.title: Repository initialized
recent_daily.summary: Empty repo created. Two months later the codex framing landed and the first cards followed.
